figures.caption: Figure 4: Schematic of the ECM-UDE model used in this work. Blocks shaded in blue denote physics-based components; the orange block denotes the learned neural correction fθ . The dashed boundary encloses the hybrid latent dynamics integrated by the solver.

Figure 8: Zero-shot temperature transfer from the UDDS 25 ◦C source domain. Filled markers denote outof-distribution target temperatures; hollow markers denote the in-distribution source condition. All three models degrade monotonically as the target temperature decreases, but ECM-UDE (blue) retains the lowest MAE at every target temperature.

Figure 6: Seed-wise validation MAE on UDDS at 25 ◦C over 30 random seeds. The horizontal dotted line marks the deterministic ECM-1RC baseline (128.0 mV). Boxes span the interquartile range; whiskers extend to the data extremes. ECM-UDE consistently outperforms the LSTM in all 30 paired comparisons (W = 0, p < 10−9).

Figure 7: Inference-time sensitivity to Gaussian noise in the z input channel on the held-out UDDS 25 ◦C validation set. The LSTM and ECM-UDE curves use the best checkpoints selected in Experiment 1; the ECM1RC baseline is deterministic.

Table 2: Consolidated summary of the main MAE results across all four evaluation settings. For matched-condition prediction, LSTM and ECM-UDE are reported as mean ± standard deviation over repeated training runs, whereas ECM-1RC is deterministic. For inference-time z perturbation, the σz = 0 row is deterministic, while the σz > 0 rows are reported as mean ± standard deviation over repeated Monte Carlo noise realizations for all methods. For temperature-transfer and drive-cycle-transfer experiments, the table reports the corresponding test-set MAE values under zero-shot transfer.
Figure 5: Representative UDDS validation segment at 25 ◦C. Measured voltage is shown in solid black; model predictions are dashed.

Figure 2: Identified OCV–SOC curve. The shaded region indicates the SOC range covered by the training data (approximately 21–100%). The curve spans approximately 3.30 V at 0% SOC to 4.15 V at 100% SOC, consistent with the NCA chemistry of the Panasonic 18650PF cell.
